lesson: c1-l03 / A Brief History of AI
title: A Brief History of AI — Why Now?

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: A brief history of AI. [pause] Today's tools didn't appear overnight. [pause] They're the result of seventy years of attempts, failures, and breakthroughs.

scenes.name: Why care
scenes.text: Why should a teacher care about AI history? [pause] Two reasons. [pause] One — it explains why AI has been talked about for decades. [pause] Two — it shows why <emphasis>this moment</emphasis> is genuinely different.

scenes.name: Timeline
scenes.text: Five turning points. [pause] 1950 — Turing asks: can machines think? [pause] 1960s — early symbolic AI like ELIZA. [pause] 1970s, 80s — two AI winters when hype crashed. [pause] 1997 — Deep Blue beats Kasparov at chess. [pause] 2022 — ChatGPT arrives, AI becomes accessible to everyone.

scenes.name: Non-linear
scenes.text: An important lesson. [pause] AI's progress has <emphasis>never</emphasis> been linear. [pause] Two winters where funding crashed. [pause] Each wave produced real advances — and inflated expectations that couldn't be met.

scenes.name: Why now
scenes.text: So why is now different? [pause] Two things changed. [pause] AI got <emphasis>better</emphasis> — large language models can hold a conversation. [pause] And AI got <emphasis>accessible</emphasis> — no coding, no setup. Just type a question.

scenes.name: ChatGPT
scenes.text: November 2022. ChatGPT released. [pause] For the first time, AI moved from a specialist topic to front-page news overnight. [pause] Teachers, students, the general public — everyone could use it the same day.

scenes.name: Practice
scenes.text: What this means for your practice. [pause] AI in education is not temporary. The teachers who navigate this transition most successfully will be those who understand the context — [pause] who know AI is powerful but imperfect, shaped by decades of effort, and still a work in progress.
